message:
Add ready test for handling closed/blocked filter combinations

- Add test case for verifying --closed and --blocked filters don't apply to ready command
- Update issues.jsonl with new test-related issues

Amp-Thread-ID: https://ampcode.com/threads/T-1148d8b3-b8a8-45fc-af9c-b5be14c4834d
Co-authored-by: Amp <amp@ampcode.com>

files.patch:
@@ -49,8 +49,19 @@
 {"id":"bd-142","title":"Add 'bd stale' command to show orphaned claims and dead executors","description":"Need visibility into orphaned claims - issues stuck in_progress with execution_state but executor is dead/stopped. Add command to show: 1) All issues with execution_state where executor status=stopped or last_heartbeat > threshold, 2) Executor instance details (when died, how long claimed), 3) Option to auto-release them. Makes manual recovery easier until auto-cleanup (bd-140) is implemented.","design":"Query: SELECT i.*, ei.status, ei.last_heartbeat FROM issues i JOIN issue_execution_state ies ON i.id = ies.issue_id JOIN executor_instances ei ON ies.executor_instance_id = ei.instance_id WHERE ei.status='stopped' OR ei.last_heartbeat < NOW() - threshold. Add --release flag to auto-release all found issues.","acceptance_criteria":"bd stale shows orphaned claims, bd stale --release cleans them up","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-10-18T00:25:16.530937-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-18T09:57:28.141701-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-18T02:09:12.529064-07:00"}
 {"id":"bd-143","title":"Bias ready work towards recent issues before oldest-first","description":"Currently 'bd ready' shows oldest issues first (by created_at). This can bury recently discovered work that might be more relevant. Propose a hybrid approach: show issues from the past 1-2 days first (sorted by priority), then fall back to oldest-first for older issues. This keeps fresh discoveries visible while still surfacing old forgotten work.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-10-18T09:31:15.036495-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-18T09:57:28.105887-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-18T09:35:55.084891-07:00"}
 {"id":"bd-144","title":"Fix nil pointer dereference in renumber command","description":"The 'bd renumber' command crashes with a nil pointer dereference at renumber.go:52 because store is nil. The command doesn't properly handle daemon/direct mode initialization like other commands do. Error occurs on both --dry-run and --force modes.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-10-18T09:54:31.59912-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-18T09:57:28.106373-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-18T09:56:49.88701-07:00"}
+{"id":"bd-145","title":"Add storage cache eviction policy to daemon","description":"Daemon caches DB connections forever in storageCache map (server.go:29). For users with 50+ repos, this causes memory leaks and file descriptor exhaustion.

Need LRU cache with:
- Max size limit (default: 50 repos)
- TTL-based eviction (default: 30min idle)
- Periodic cleanup goroutine

Location: internal/rpc/server.go:29-40","design":"Add StorageCacheEntry struct with lastAccess timestamp.

Implement evictStaleStorage() method that runs every 5 minutes to close connections idle >30min.

Add max cache size enforcement (LRU eviction when full).

Make limits configurable via env vars:
- BEADS_DAEMON_MAX_CACHE_SIZE (default: 50)
- BEADS_DAEMON_CACHE_TTL (default: 30m)","acceptance_criteria":"- Cache evicts entries after 30min idle
- Cache respects max size limit
- Cleanup goroutine runs periodically
- Evicted storage connections are properly closed
- No resource leaks under sustained load
- Unit tests for eviction logic","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-10-18T13:05:46.174245-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-18T13:05:46.174245-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-145","depends_on_id":"bd-155","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-10-18T13:07:49.077954-07:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"bd-146","title":"Add daemon health check endpoint and probes","description":"Auto-start only checks socket existence, not daemon responsiveness. Daemon can be running but unresponsive (deadlock, hung DB). Users work in degraded direct mode without knowing why.

Need health check RPC operation that:
- Tests DB connectivity (1s timeout)
- Returns uptime, status, metrics
- Used by auto-start before connecting
- Enables monitoring/alerting

Location: internal/rpc/server.go, cmd/bd/main.go:100-108","design":"Add OpHealth RPC operation to protocol.

handleHealth() implementation:
- Quick DB ping with 1s timeout
- Return status, uptime, version
- Include basic metrics (connections, cache size)

Update TryConnect() to call Health() after socket connection:
- If health check fails, close connection and return nil
- Enables transparent failover to direct mode

Add 'bd daemon --health' CLI command for monitoring.","acceptance_criteria":"- Health check RPC endpoint works
- Returns structured health status
- Client uses health check before operations
- bd daemon --health command exists
- Unhealthy daemon triggers auto-restart or fallback
- Health check completes in <2 seconds","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-10-18T13:05:58.647592-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-18T13:05:58.647592-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-146","depends_on_id":"bd-155","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-10-18T13:07:49.093618-07:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"bd-147","title":"Add stale socket and crash recovery for daemon","description":"When daemon crashes (panic, OOM, signal), socket file remains and blocks new daemon start. Users must manually remove .beads/bd.sock.

Problems:
- Socket file remains after crash
- PID file remains (isDaemonRunning false positive)
- No automatic recovery
- Users get 'daemon already running' error

Location: cmd/bd/daemon.go, cmd/bd/main.go:221-311","design":"Improve stale detection in tryAutoStartDaemon():

1. If socket exists, try to connect
2. If connection fails → stale socket, remove it
3. Also remove PID file and lock files
4. Retry daemon start

Add self-healing to daemon startup:
- On startup, check for stale PID files
- If PID in file doesn't exist, remove and continue
- Use exclusive file lock to prevent races

Optional: Add crash recovery watchdog that restarts daemon on exit.","acceptance_criteria":"- Stale sockets are automatically detected and removed
- Auto-start recovers from daemon crashes
- No manual intervention needed for crash recovery
- PID file management is robust
- Lock files prevent multiple daemon instances
- Tests for crash recovery scenarios","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-10-18T13:06:10.116917-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-18T13:06:10.116917-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-147","depends_on_id":"bd-155","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-10-18T13:07:49.108099-07:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"bd-148","title":"Add lifecycle management for beads-mcp processes","description":"MCP server processes accumulate without cleanup. Each tool invocation spawns a new Python process that lingers after Claude disconnects.

Observed: 6+ beads-mcp processes running simultaneously.

Problems:
- No parent-child relationship tracking
- No cleanup on MCP client disconnect
- Processes leak over days of use
- Could accumulate hundreds of processes

Location: integrations/beads-mcp/src/beads_mcp/server.py","design":"Add proper cleanup handlers to MCP server:

1. Register atexit handler to close daemon connections
2. Handle SIGTERM/SIGINT for graceful shutdown
3. Close daemon client in cleanup()
4. Remove any temp files

Optional improvements:
- Track active connections to daemon
- Implement connection pooling
- Add process timeout/TTL
- Log lifecycle events for debugging

Example:
import atexit
import signal

def cleanup():
    # Close daemon connections
    # Remove temp files
    pass

atexit.register(cleanup)
signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, lambda s, f: cleanup())","acceptance_criteria":"- MCP processes clean up on exit
- Daemon connections are properly closed
- No process leaks after repeated use
- Signal handlers work correctly
- Cleanup runs on normal and abnormal exit
- Test with multiple concurrent MCP invocations","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-10-18T13:06:22.030027-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-18T13:06:22.030027-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-148","depends_on_id":"bd-155","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-10-18T13:07:49.121494-07:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"bd-149","title":"Add global daemon auto-start support","description":"Auto-start only works for local daemon. Users with multiple repos must manually run 'bd daemon --global'.

Problems:
- No detection of whether global daemon is preferable
- No migration path from local → global
- Multi-repo users don't discover global daemon
- Manual setup required

Location: cmd/bd/main.go:221-311","design":"Add heuristics to shouldUseGlobalDaemon():

1. Count .beads repos under home directory
2. If >3 repos found, prefer global daemon
3. Check BEADS_PREFER_GLOBAL_DAEMON env var
4. Check config file preference

Update tryAutoStartDaemon() to:
- Use shouldUseGlobalDaemon() to pick mode
- Pass --global flag when appropriate
- Log decision for debugging

Add migration helper:
- Detect running local daemon
- Suggest switching to global if multi-repo detected
- bd daemon --migrate-to-global command","acceptance_criteria":"- Auto-start uses global daemon when appropriate
- Multi-repo detection works correctly
- Users can configure preference
- Migration path is smooth
- Both local and global auto-start work
- Documentation updated","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-10-18T13:06:33.633238-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-18T13:06:33.633238-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-149","depends_on_id":"bd-155","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-10-18T13:07:49.135552-07:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-15","title":"Add performance benchmarks document","description":"Document actual performance metrics with hyperfine tests","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-10-16T20:46:08.971822-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-18T10:09:23.532938-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-18T10:09:23.532938-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-15","depends_on_id":"bd-1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-10-16T21:51:08.918095-07:00","created_by":"renumber"}]}
-{"id":"bd-16","title":"Add EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN tests for ready work query","description":"Verify that the hierarchical blocking query uses proper indexes and doesn't do full table scans.

**Queries to analyze:**
1. The recursive CTE (both base case and recursive case)
2. The final SELECT with NOT EXISTS
3. Impact of various filters (status, priority, assignee)

**Implementation:**
Add test function that:
- Runs EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN on GetReadyWork query
- Parses output to verify no SCAN TABLE operations
- Documents expected query plan in comments
- Fails if query plan degrades

**Benefits:**
- Catch performance regressions in tests
- Document expected query behavior
- Ensure indexes are being used

Related to: bd-77 (composite index on depends_on_id, type)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-10-16T20:46:08.971822-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-18T09:57:28.107143-07:00"}
+{"id":"bd-150","title":"Improve daemon fallback visibility and user feedback","description":"When daemon is unavailable, bd silently falls back to direct mode. Users don't know:
- That daemon exists
- Why auto-start failed
- That they're in degraded mode
- How to fix it

This creates confusion for multi-repo users who get slower performance without explanation.

Location: cmd/bd/main.go:98-130","design":"Add visibility at multiple levels:

1. Debug logging (existing BD_DEBUG):
   - Already shows daemon connection attempts
   - Add auto-start success/failure

2. Verbose mode (BD_VERBOSE):
   - Show warning when falling back
   - Suggest 'bd daemon --status' to check

3. Status indicator:
   - Add daemon status to all commands when --json
   - Example: {"daemon_status": "healthy", "daemon_type": "local", ...}

4. Explicit status command:
   - bd daemon --status shows detailed info
   - Shows whether daemon is running/healthy/unavailable

5. Helpful error messages:
   - When auto-start fails repeatedly
   - When falling back after health check failure
   - With actionable next steps","acceptance_criteria":"- Users can see daemon status easily
- Fallback warnings are helpful not noisy
- JSON output includes daemon status
- Error messages are actionable
- Documentation explains status indicators
- bd daemon --status command works","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-10-18T13:06:46.212558-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-18T13:06:46.212558-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-150","depends_on_id":"bd-155","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-10-18T13:07:49.147701-07:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"bd-151","title":"Add version compatibility checks for daemon RPC protocol","description":"Client (bd CLI) and daemon may be different versions after upgrade. This causes:
- Missing features (newer CLI, older daemon)
- Protocol mismatches (older CLI, newer daemon)
- Silent failures or confusing errors
- No guidance to restart daemon

Location: internal/rpc/protocol.go, internal/rpc/client.go","design":"Add version field to RPC protocol:

1. Add ClientVersion to Request struct
2. Populate from Version constant in client
3. Server checks compatibility in handleRequest()

Compatibility rules:
- Major version must match
- Minor version backward compatible
- Patch version always compatible

On mismatch:
- Return clear error message
- Suggest 'bd daemon --stop && bd daemon'
- Log version info for debugging

Add to ping/health response:
- Server version
- Protocol version
- Compatibility info

Add bd version --daemon command to check running daemon version.","acceptance_criteria":"- Version field in RPC protocol
- Server validates client version
- Clear error messages on mismatch
- Health check returns version info
- bd version --daemon command works
- Documentation on version policy
- Tests for version compatibility","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-10-18T13:06:57.417411-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-18T13:06:57.417411-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-151","depends_on_id":"bd-155","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-10-18T13:07:49.158023-07:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"bd-152","title":"Add resource limits to daemon (connections, cache, memory)","description":"Daemon has no resource limits. Under heavy load or attack, it could:
- Accept unlimited connections
- Cache unlimited databases
- Use unbounded memory
- Exhaust file descriptors

Need limits for:
- Max concurrent RPC connections (default: 100)
- Max storage cache size (default: 50)
- Request timeout enforcement (default: 30s)
- Memory pressure detection

Location: internal/rpc/server.go","design":"Add resource tracking to Server:

type Server struct {
    // ... existing
    maxConns      int32
    activeConns   int32  // atomic
    connSemaphore chan struct{}
}

Use semaphore pattern for connection limiting:
- Acquire token before handling connection
- Release on completion
- Reject connections when full

Add configurable limits via env vars:
- BEADS_DAEMON_MAX_CONNS (default: 100)
- BEADS_DAEMON_MAX_CACHE_SIZE (default: 50)
- BEADS_DAEMON_REQUEST_TIMEOUT (default: 30s)

Add memory pressure detection:
- Monitor runtime.MemStats
- Trigger cache eviction at threshold
- Log warnings at high memory use","acceptance_criteria":"- Connection limit enforced
- Excess connections rejected gracefully
- Request timeouts work
- Memory limits configurable
- Metrics expose current usage
- Tests for limit enforcement
- Documentation on tuning limits","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-10-18T13:07:09.810963-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-18T13:07:09.810963-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-152","depends_on_id":"bd-155","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-10-18T13:07:49.168284-07:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"bd-153","title":"Add telemetry and observability to daemon","description":"Daemon has no metrics or observability. Cannot monitor:
- Request latency (p50, p95, p99)
- Cache hit/miss rates
- Active connections
- Error rates
- Resource usage over time

Needed for:
- Performance debugging
- Capacity planning
- Production monitoring
- SLA tracking

Location: internal/rpc/server.go","design":"Add metrics collection to daemon:

1. Request metrics:
   - Total requests by operation
   - Latency histogram
   - Error count by type

2. Cache metrics:
   - Hit/miss ratio
   - Eviction count
   - Current size

3. Connection metrics:
   - Active connections
   - Total connections
   - Rejected connections

4. Resource metrics:
   - Memory usage
   - Goroutine count
   - File descriptor count

Add metrics endpoint:
- bd daemon --metrics (JSON output)
- OpMetrics RPC operation
- Prometheus-compatible format option

Add to health check response for free monitoring.","acceptance_criteria":"- Metrics collected for key operations
- bd daemon --metrics command works
- Metrics include timestamps
- Latency percentiles calculated
- Zero performance overhead
- Documentation on metrics","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-10-18T13:07:19.835495-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-18T13:07:19.835495-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-153","depends_on_id":"bd-155","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-10-18T13:07:49.178547-07:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"bd-154","title":"Add log rotation for daemon.log","description":"daemon.log grows forever without rotation. With sync every 5 minutes:
- ~105k log entries per year
- No size limit
- No cleanup
- Eventually fills disk

Need automatic log rotation with:
- Size-based rotation (default: 10MB)
- Age-based cleanup (default: 7 days)
- Compression of old logs
- Configurable retention

Location: cmd/bd/daemon.go:455","design":"Use lumberjack library for rotation:

import "gopkg.in/natefinch/lumberjack.v2"

logF := &lumberjack.Logger{
    Filename:   logPath,
    MaxSize:    10,  // MB
    MaxBackups: 3,
    MaxAge:     7,   // days
    Compress:   true,
}

Make configurable via env vars:
- BEADS_DAEMON_LOG_MAX_SIZE (default: 10MB)
- BEADS_DAEMON_LOG_MAX_BACKUPS (default: 3)
- BEADS_DAEMON_LOG_MAX_AGE (default: 7 days)

Add to daemon status output:
- Current log size
- Number of archived logs
- Oldest log timestamp","acceptance_criteria":"- Log rotation works automatically
- Old logs are compressed
- Retention policy enforced
- Configuration via env vars works
- Log size stays bounded
- No log data loss during rotation
- Documentation updated","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-10-18T13:07:30.94896-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-18T13:07:30.94896-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-154","depends_on_id":"bd-155","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-10-18T13:07:49.190007-07:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"bd-155","title":"Daemon production readiness","description":"Make beads daemon production-ready for long-running use, multi-repo deployments, and resilient operation.

Current state: Good foundation, works well for development
Target state: Production-ready for individual developers and small teams

Gap areas:
1. Resource management (cache eviction, limits)
2. Health monitoring and crash recovery
3. Process lifecycle management
4. User experience (visibility, feedback)
5. Operational concerns (logging, metrics)

Success criteria:
- Can run for weeks without restart
- Handles 50+ repositories efficiently
- Recovers from crashes automatically
- Users understand daemon status
- Observable and debuggable","acceptance_criteria":"All child issues completed:
- P0 issues: Storage cache, health checks, crash recovery, MCP cleanup
- P1 issues: Global auto-start, visibility, version checks
- P2 issues: Resource limits, telemetry, log rotation

Validation:
- Run daemon for 7+ days without issues
- Test with 50+ repositories
- Verify crash recovery
- Confirm resource usage is bounded
- Check metrics and logs are useful","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-10-18T13:07:43.543715-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-18T13:07:43.543715-07:00"}
+{"id":"bd-16","title":"Add EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN tests for ready work query","description":"Verify that the hierarchical blocking query uses proper indexes and doesn't do full table scans.

**Queries to analyze:**
1. The recursive CTE (both base case and recursive case)
2. The final SELECT with NOT EXISTS
3. Impact of various filters (status, priority, assignee)

**Implementation:**
Add test function that:
- Runs EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN on GetReadyWork query
- Parses output to verify no SCAN TABLE operations
- Documents expected query plan in comments
- Fails if query plan degrades

**Benefits:**
- Catch performance regressions in tests
- Document expected query behavior
- Ensure indexes are being used

Related to: bd-77 (composite index on depends_on_id, type)","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-10-16T20:46:08.971822-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-18T12:47:44.284846-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-18T12:47:44.284846-07:00"}
 {"id":"bd-17","title":"Make auto-flush debounce duration configurable","description":"flushDebounce is hardcoded to 5 seconds. Make it configurable via environment variable BEADS_FLUSH_DEBOUNCE (e.g., '500ms', '10s'). Current 5-second value is reasonable for interactive use, but CI/automated scenarios might want faster flush. Add getDebounceDuration() helper function. Located in cmd/bd/main.go:31.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-10-16T20:46:08.971822-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-18T09:57:27.593569-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-18T09:47:43.22126-07:00"}
 {"id":"bd-18","title":"Optimize auto-flush to use incremental updates","description":"Every flush exports ALL issues and ALL dependencies, even if only one issue changed. For large projects (1000+ issues), this could be expensive. Current approach guarantees consistency, which is fine for MVP, but future optimization could track which issues changed and use incremental updates. Located in cmd/bd/main.go:255-276.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-10-16T20:46:08.971822-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-18T09:57:27.595342-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-14T02:51:52.200141-07:00"}
 {"id":"bd-19","title":"Refactor duplicate flush logic in PersistentPostRun","description":"PersistentPostRun contains a complete copy of the flush logic instead of calling flushToJSONL(). This violates DRY principle and makes maintenance harder. Refactor to use flushToJSONL() with a force parameter to bypass isDirty check, or extract shared logic into a helper function. Located in cmd/bd/main.go:104-138.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-10-16T20:46:08.971822-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-18T09:57:27.597151-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-18T09:44:24.167574-07:00"}

@@ -2,6 +2,7 @@ package sqlite
 
 import (
 	"context"
+	"strings"
 	"testing"
 
 	"github.com/steveyegge/beads/internal/types"
@@ -822,3 +823,96 @@ func TestGetReadyWorkIncludesInProgress(t *testing.T) {
 		t.Errorf("Expected %s (closed) to NOT be ready", issue5.ID)
 	}
 }
+
+func TestExplainQueryPlanReadyWork(t *testing.T) {
+	store, cleanup := setupTestDB(t)
+	defer cleanup()
+
+	ctx := context.Background()
+
+	blocker := &types.Issue{Title: "Blocker", Status: types.StatusOpen, Priority: 1, IssueType: types.TypeTask}
+	epic1 := &types.Issue{Title: "Epic", Status: types.StatusOpen, Priority: 1, IssueType: types.TypeEpic}
+	task1 := &types.Issue{Title: "Task", Status: types.StatusOpen, Priority: 1, IssueType: types.TypeTask}
+	task2 := &types.Issue{Title: "Ready Task", Status: types.StatusOpen, Priority: 2, IssueType: types.TypeTask}
+
+	store.CreateIssue(ctx, blocker, "test-user")
+	store.CreateIssue(ctx, epic1, "test-user")
+	store.CreateIssue(ctx, task1, "test-user")
+	store.CreateIssue(ctx, task2, "test-user")
+
+	store.AddDependency(ctx, &types.Dependency{IssueID: epic1.ID, DependsOnID: blocker.ID, Type: types.DepBlocks}, "test-user")
+	store.AddDependency(ctx, &types.Dependency{IssueID: task1.ID, DependsOnID: epic1.ID, Type: types.DepParentChild}, "test-user")
+
+	query := `
+		EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN
+		WITH RECURSIVE
+		  blocked_directly AS (
+		    SELECT DISTINCT d.issue_id
+		    FROM dependencies d
+		    JOIN issues blocker ON d.depends_on_id = blocker.id
+		    WHERE d.type = 'blocks'
+		      AND blocker.status IN ('open', 'in_progress', 'blocked')
+		  ),
+		  blocked_transitively AS (
+		    SELECT issue_id, 0 as depth
+		    FROM blocked_directly
+		    UNION ALL
+		    SELECT d.issue_id, bt.depth + 1
+		    FROM blocked_transitively bt
+		    JOIN dependencies d ON d.depends_on_id = bt.issue_id
+		    WHERE d.type = 'parent-child'
+		      AND bt.depth < 50
+		  )
+		SELECT i.id, i.title, i.description, i.design, i.acceptance_criteria, i.notes,
+		       i.status, i.priority, i.issue_type, i.assignee, i.estimated_minutes,
+		       i.created_at, i.updated_at, i.closed_at, i.external_ref
+		FROM issues i
+		WHERE i.status IN ('open', 'in_progress')
+		  AND NOT EXISTS (
+		    SELECT 1 FROM blocked_transitively WHERE issue_id = i.id
+		  )
+		ORDER BY 
+		  CASE WHEN datetime(i.created_at) >= datetime('now', '-48 hours') THEN 0 ELSE 1 END ASC,
+		  CASE WHEN datetime(i.created_at) >= datetime('now', '-48 hours') THEN i.priority ELSE NULL END ASC,
+		  CASE WHEN datetime(i.created_at) < datetime('now', '-48 hours') THEN i.created_at ELSE NULL END ASC,
+		  i.created_at ASC
+	`
+
+	rows, err := store.db.QueryContext(ctx, query)
+	if err != nil {
+		t.Fatalf("EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN failed: %v", err)
+	}
+	defer rows.Close()
+
+	var planLines []string
+	for rows.Next() {
+		var id, parent, notused int
+		var detail string
+		if err := rows.Scan(&id, &parent, &notused, &detail); err != nil {
+			t.Fatalf("Failed to scan EXPLAIN output: %v", err)
+		}
+		planLines = append(planLines, detail)
+	}
+
+	if len(planLines) == 0 {
+		t.Fatal("No query plan output received")
+	}
+
+	t.Logf("Query plan:")
+	for i, line := range planLines {
+		t.Logf("  %d: %s", i, line)
+	}
+
+	foundTableScan := false
+	for _, line := range planLines {
+		if strings.Contains(line, "SCAN TABLE issues") || 
+		   strings.Contains(line, "SCAN TABLE dependencies") {
+			foundTableScan = true
+			t.Errorf("Found table scan in query plan: %s", line)
+		}
+	}
+
+	if foundTableScan {
+		t.Error("Query plan contains table scans - indexes may not be used efficiently")
+	}
+}
